access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q25.explanation: Câu 25 khớp với thông tin ở đoạn C. Ý chính trong bài là: "As the industrial revolution took hold in the United States, the demand for cans increased and machines began to replace the artisans’ handiwork. A good artisan could make only 10 cans a day. True production progress in can making began in 1922, when American engineers perfected the body-making process, and soon production increased to as many as 250 cans a minute.". Vì câu hỏi diễn đạt lại cùng một ý, đáp án là TRUE.
q25.evidence: As the industrial revolution took hold in the United States, the demand for cans increased and machines began to replace the artisans’ handiwork. A good artisan could make only 10 cans a day. True production progress in can making began in 1922, when American engineers perfected the body-making process, and soon production increased to as many as 250 cans a minute._

q26.explanation: Câu 26 có một số từ khóa gần với đoạn F, nhưng bài không cung cấp đủ chi tiết để xác nhận hay phủ định toàn bộ mệnh đề. Câu gần nhất là: "By the 1980s and 1990s, recycling had become a way of life. Aluminium can recycling has become a billion-dollar business and one of the world’s most successful environmental enterprises. Over the years, the aluminium can has come to be known as America’s most recyclable package, with over 60 per cent of cans being recycled annually.". Vì thiếu thông tin trực tiếp, đáp án là NOT GIVEN.
q26.evidence: By the 1980s and 1990s, recycling had become a way of life. Aluminium can recycling has become a billion-dollar business and one of the world’s most successful environmental enterprises. Over the years, the aluminium can has come to be known as America’s most recyclable package, with over 60 per cent of cans being recycled annually._
